creator: jkois@freebsd.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

Hm,

As far as I know the "FreeBSD version number at the footer" is the
version of the system where the manpages where built and NOT the version
you searching for.  So even if you are searching for a 9-CURRENT manpage
it will still say "FreeBSD 7.2".  But the manpage should be the same as
the one you have on your local system.  See also:

http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-doc/2010-August/017461.html

Could you check if the manualpage is really outdated?  Meaning different
from the one you have on your local 9-CURRENT system?

Because here (8.1-RELEASE) the content of the manpage is the same as on
FreeBSD.org.  Only the footer is different (here it says "FreeBSD 8.1"
as this is the FreeBSD version I am using).


- -- 
 Johann Kois
 jkois(at)FreeBSD.org
 FreeBSD Documentation Project
 FreeBSD German Documentation Project - https://doc.bsdgroup.de
